title: asperula oppositifolia subsp. chitralensis
intro: Asperula oppositifolia subsp. chitralensis, also known as Chitral asperula, is a species of plant in the Rubiaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Asperula oppositifolia subsp. chitralensis include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic, making it useful for treating various ailments such as diarrhea and respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
